claimBoundary.reason: Blocked, but P67-B prep landed. The judge-free deterministic-subset ANALYZER is now built and unit-tested (scripts/run-phase-62-deterministic-subset.ts, alias eval:phase-62-deterministic-subset; pure summarizeLongMemEvalDeterministicSubset + 6 passing tests). It carves out the strict judge-free lower bound: a case counts correct only when scored by a deterministic method (abstention/exact/contains/expected_alternative/numeric_count); semantic_judge is excluded by construction (verified against the scorer in src/eval/longmemeval.ts, which only invokes the judge when every deterministic method returns mismatch). The planned public metric is goodmemory-hybrid deterministic-subset accuracy with baseline-no-memory (computed in the same run) as the judge-free baseline + memory-lift disclosure. The metrics block above (0.908) is the OLD with-judge number and is NOT the claim. Remaining blockers, all judge-INDEPENDENT now: (1) a fresh v0.3 full-500 run is required — BLOCKED on the gpt-5.5 endpoint (probed 2026-06-27, 503 no-distributor); (2) executionFailures must be driven to 0 via eval:phase-62-full500-retry-failures; (3) dataset.license must be verified upstream; (4) run.commit + run.packageVersion stamped from the fresh run. On a clean run the metric reframes to judge-free (judgeModel null, sameModelJudge false) and the gate is re-evaluated.
